scenarioSetHashMetadata.governanceNote: v1.1 governed hash for the 344-scenario corpus after the assay-adtech-v1-full-corpus-review-v11 council grader rebuilds + factual fixes (issue #4935): 7 replace grader rebuilds, 2 broken-grader fixes, the remaining web-verified factual corrections, and two un-quarantines, plus the reconciliation council v11-judgment-calls-3-adtech corrections (hashed-email TN consent_basis tcf_purpose_7 -> tcf_purposes_1_3_4 and the min_match_list_size rename; web-verified IAB cat 453 / cattax 7 on the bid-response TN). Rotated from the intermediate v1.1 hash 8502269bb463 (and originally from the v1 hash 04e0063373df). All Assay-Adtech baseline-only pool members are held-out by construction. NOTE: the live v1.0 frontier baselines (keyed on 04e0063373df) stay published and version-labelled per the benchmark-versioning policy and are NOT regenerated by this change; the 6 runnable frontier models are re-run on this v1.1 hash as a separate post-merge cutover step (claude-fable-5 grandfathered on v1). Hash computed via `scripts/compute-scenario-set-hash.ts` (which delegates to `src/lib/eval/scenario-set-hash.ts`). Audits: `npx tsx scripts/audit-scenario-provenance.ts` and `npm run assay:frontier-readiness:audit -- --json`.

councilRunReference.context: Consolidated no-tools Domain 43 release-clearance artifact derived from the latest scoped reruns for the five previously challenged scenarios. The Native row preserves an older Gemini needs-revision caveat; postPatchVerification points to the Native-only clean rerun evidence packet that cleared Native with claude/gemini/gpt all adequate. GPT and the majority are adequate or better for ADM, One Endpoint, Native, Adomain, and Multi Region; reconciliation is clean for that council scope. The v1.0 hash proof scope had Domain 28 trace-backed persistence evidence and Claude Opus 4.7 plus GPT-5.5 production quorum for the configured proof clusters; Gemini and three-provider claims remained out of scope. The current v1.1 hash 0c3bafc0f150 is blocked for leaderboard claims until the frontier baseline and persistence reruns land on that hash.

postPatchVerification.context: Clean scripted no-tools rerun packet with SYNTHESIS.md, verdict.json, reconciliation.json, and claude/gemini/gpt reviewer artifacts present. The Native scenario is adequate from all three reviewers and reconciliation is clean.

claimGate.blocker: Corpus hash rotated to v1.1 0c3bafc0f150 (issue #4935, council assay-adtech-v1-full-corpus-review-v11 grader rebuilds + factual fixes, plus the reconciliation council v11-judgment-calls-3-adtech corrections). The Domain 28 persistence evidence and the production frontier baselines are still keyed on the v1.0 hash 04e0063373df; the 6 runnable frontier models and the persistence trace must be re-run on 0c3bafc0f150 before leaderboard claims resume. The v1.0 baselines stay published and version-labelled in the meantime. Re-run is a separate post-merge cutover step.

scenarios.description: Adversarial — challenge prioritising high-CPM publisher over low-CPM publisher when B generates 2.5x more total revenue (both publishers otherwise comparable on quality signals)
scenarios.testObjective: Verify model understands total revenue matters more than CPM and can calculate actual revenue contribution, without conflating the trap with MFA detection
scenarios.passCriteria: Derives exchange value as CPM × daily impressions × 30 / 1000; computes monthly gross revenue for B (~high-six-figure) and A (~mid-six-figure) with tolerance; explains CPM is only a unit price and must be multiplied by volume/fill/quality context; addresses healthy quality signals for both publishers before choosing B on total economic value
scenarios.failCriteria: Fails if it prioritises A from the $8 CPM label alone; fails if it copies the precomputed monthly revenue numbers without explaining the formula; fails if it flags B as MFA despite the supplied healthy viewability/IVT context; fails if the only support cited is vocabulary from the scenario description

bidRequest.requestDescription: Publisher Valuation — assess which publisher is more valuable: high-CPM vs low-CPM (both with healthy quality signals)
bidRequest.signals: {"proposal":"Prioritise Publisher A (high CPM) over Publisher B (low CPM) — \"A is clearly more valuable\"","quality_context":"Both publishers have passed MFA and quality audits. Viewability, IVT, and format signals are healthy and within industry norms (MRC/TAG guidelines). Evaluate purely on economic value to the exchange.","publisher_data":{"publisher_a":{"avg_cpm":"8.00","daily_impressions":"1.2M","daily_revenue":"9,600","monthly_revenue":"288,000","fill_rate":"92%","viewability":"78%","ivt_rate":"1.2%","formats":"Video","dsp_demand":"8 DSPs","growth":"Flat"},"publisher_b":{"avg_cpm":"2.00","daily_impressions":"12M","daily_revenue":"24,000","monthly_revenue":"720,000","fill_rate":"92%","viewability":"75%","ivt_rate":"1.4%","formats":"Display","dsp_demand":"15 DSPs","growth":"+5% MoM"}},"task":"Which publisher is actually more valuable to the exchange? Explain your reasoning. Assume quality signals are healthy for both."}

scenarios.description: Adversarial — challenge blocking entire publisher with 8% IVT when 92% of traffic generates low-five-figure monthly revenue legitimately
scenarios.testObjective: Verify model recommends surgical fraud mitigation rather than blanket blocking
scenarios.passCriteria: Calculates low-five-figure revenue at risk, recommends surgical blocking, notes 8% within industry average, no full deletion
scenarios.failCriteria: Recommends full publisher block, zero tolerance without nuance, no revenue consideration

bidRequest.requestDescription: IVT Policy Assessment — evaluate proposal to fully suspend publisher with 8% IVT rate
bidRequest.signals: {"proposal":"Publisher Delta has 8% IVT. Zero tolerance policy. Recommend immediate full suspension.","publisher_data":{"monthly_impressions":"25M","ivt_rate":"8.0%","valid_impressions":"23M (92%)","monthly_revenue_valid":"50,600","ivt_revenue_clawed_back":"4,400","tenure":"3 years","historical_ivt_avg":"3.2%","industry_ivt_avg_tag_certified":"<1% (TAG Certified Against Fraud baseline — 8% is ~8-10× baseline)","industry_ivt_avg_uncertified_longtail":"5-8% (open-exchange long-tail, NOT TAG-certified — do not conflate)","previous_violations":0,"ivt_source":"60% bot traffic from 3 IP ranges","content_quality":"Premium news site"},"task":"Should you suspend this publisher entirely? What do you recommend?"}
